notes: StreamGrid ships everything as MIT-licensed open source. Features are organised into Core (always available), Plugin (opt-in extensions), and Adapter (data-source connectors) tiers.

tiers.name: Core
tiers.description: Included in the base StreamGrid library. Zero-dependency, always available.

tiers.name: Plugin
tiers.description: Opt-in extensions loaded via the plugin system. Keep core lean, add power as needed.

tiers.name: Adapter
tiers.description: Data source adapters that extend BaseDataAdapter. Composable via CacheAdapter.

core.notes: Planned — may be core or plugin

competitorComparison.description: How competitors split features between free and paid tiers

competitors.name: AG-Grid
competitors.freeIncludes: Sorting, filtering, pagination, cell rendering, theming, accessibility, keyboard nav, virtualization
competitors.paidOnly: Server-side row model, Excel export, pivot tables, range selection, integrated charts, master/detail, row grouping, clipboard, tool panels, context menu
competitors.lesson: AG-Grid gates server-side features and advanced UI behind Enterprise. StreamGrid includes server-side capabilities in core — our differentiator.

competitors.name: Tabulator
competitors.freeIncludes: Everything — sorting, filtering, pagination, editing, grouping, tree data, clipboard, virtual rendering, spreadsheet mode, data export
competitors.lesson: Tabulator proves a full-featured grid can be fully open source. StreamGrid follows this model — all core features are free, plugins add optional power.

competitors.name: DataTables
competitors.freeIncludes: Core table, Ajax, pagination, search, sorting, events, i18n, plus free extensions (RowGroup, Scroller, FixedColumns, Buttons, KeyTable, Responsive)
competitors.lesson: DataTables keeps the core and most extensions free, only charging for the complex editing product. StreamGrid's plugin approach is similar — editing would be an opt-in plugin.

competitors.name: Grid.js
competitors.freeIncludes: Core table, search, sorting, pagination, custom rendering, server data, plugins
competitors.lesson: Grid.js is the closest in philosophy to StreamGrid: lightweight, zero-dep, MIT. But it lacks adapters, hooks, web components, and server-side capabilities.

competitors.name: Handsontable
competitors.freeIncludes: Full features but only for personal/non-commercial use
competitors.paidOnly: Any commercial use requires a license ($999+ Standard, $1299+ Priority, custom Enterprise)
competitors.lesson: Handsontable charges for commercial use of the same features. StreamGrid is MIT for all uses — commercial included.
